title: Bug: Unified Graphiti search/delete contract is internally inconsistent
analysis:
Three interrelated bugs in the fused-memory unified API:

1. **ID type mismatch in delete contract**: `_search_graphiti` returns edge UUIDs (EntityEdge.uuid) as the `id` field on MemoryResult, but `delete_memory(store='graphiti')` calls `graphiti.remove_episode(memory_id)` which expects an EpisodicNode UUID. Passing an edge UUID to remove_episode would raise an error or delete the wrong thing. The MCP tool docs promise 'The memory_id and store values come from search results' — this contract is broken for Graphiti.

2. **Category filter silently drops ALL Graphiti results**: `_search_graphiti` (line 467) hardcodes `category=None` on every result. The `search()` category filter at line 423 (`[r for r in results if r.category in cat_set]`) then excludes every Graphiti result since None is never in the category set. Users filtering by 'entities_and_relations' (a Graphiti-primary category) get zero Graphiti results.

3. **No category recovery for Graphiti edges**: At write time, categories route to Graphiti (entities_and_relations, temporal_facts, decisions_and_rationale via GRAPHITI_PRIMARY). At search time, this mapping is lost because Graphiti edges don't carry category metadata. The source_description on the episode has it (e.g. 'add_memory:entities_and_relations') but that's not accessible on the edge object returned by search.

**Fix approach**:
- Add `remove_edge` to GraphitiBackend using `EntityEdge.get_by_uuid` + `edge.delete` (confirmed available in graphiti_core).
- Change `delete_memory(store='graphiti')` to delete edges instead of episodes. Episode deletion stays in the dedicated `delete_episode` tool.
- Fix category filter: when requested categories overlap with GRAPHITI_PRIMARY, include Graphiti results (category=None) rather than silently dropping them. When exactly one GRAPHITI_PRIMARY category is requested, assign it to the Graphiti results for best-effort category metadata.
- Update MCP tool docs to accurately describe what Graphiti search IDs represent (edge UUIDs, not episode UUIDs).

prerequisites.description: Extend MockEdge in conftest.py with uuid, source_node, target_node, episodes, valid_at, invalid_at attributes for realistic Graphiti search result mocking in new tests

steps.description: Write failing test: test_search_category_filter_includes_graphiti_when_graphiti_primary_requested — mock Graphiti returning edges (via extended MockEdge with uuid) and Mem0 returning results, filter by ['entities_and_relations'], assert Graphiti results are NOT dropped. Currently fails because category=None is filtered out by the list comprehension at line 423.

Write failing test: test_search_category_filter_excludes_graphiti_when_only_mem0_primary_requested — filter by ['preferences_and_norms'] only, mock both stores returning results, assert Graphiti results (category=None) ARE correctly excluded since none of the requested categories are in GRAPHITI_PRIMARY. This test should pass after the fix but documents the expected behavior.

Fix category filter logic in MemoryService.search() (line 420-423): when the requested categories overlap with GRAPHITI_PRIMARY, include Graphiti results that have category=None. Change filter from `r.category in cat_set` to also accept `(r.source_store == SourceStore.graphiti and r.category is None and cat_set & GRAPHITI_PRIMARY)`. Import GRAPHITI_PRIMARY is already at line 18.

Write failing test: test_search_graphiti_results_get_inferred_category_when_single_primary — when category filter has exactly one GRAPHITI_PRIMARY category (e.g. ['entities_and_relations']), verify the returned Graphiti results have that category assigned (not left as None). This helps downstream consumers know what category was requested.

Enhance category filter to assign inferred category to Graphiti results: after filtering, for Graphiti results with category=None, if exactly one GRAPHITI_PRIMARY category is in the filter set, assign it. When multiple GRAPHITI_PRIMARY categories are in the filter, leave as None (ambiguous). This provides best-effort category info without false precision.

Write failing test: test_graphiti_backend_remove_edge — unit test for new GraphitiBackend.remove_edge(edge_uuid) method. Mock EntityEdge.get_by_uuid to return a mock edge, verify edge.delete is called with the driver. Method doesn't exist yet so this fails with AttributeError.

Add remove_edge method to GraphitiBackend (graphiti_client.py): import EntityEdge from graphiti_core.edges, implement remove_edge(edge_uuid) using get_by_uuid + edge.delete with asyncio.wait_for timeout wrapper, same pattern as existing remove_episode (lines 205-212).

Write failing test: test_delete_memory_graphiti_calls_remove_edge — verify that MemoryService.delete_memory(memory_id='edge-uuid', store='graphiti') calls self.graphiti.remove_edge('edge-uuid') instead of self.graphiti.remove_episode('edge-uuid'). Add remove_edge mock to the service fixture. Currently fails because delete_memory calls remove_episode.

Change MemoryService.delete_memory (line 583) to call self.graphiti.remove_edge(memory_id) instead of self.graphiti.remove_episode(memory_id) when source is SourceStore.graphiti. The delete_episode method continues to use remove_episode.

Write regression test: test_delete_episode_still_uses_remove_episode — verify MemoryService.delete_episode continues to call self.graphiti.remove_episode (not remove_edge). Should pass immediately as a regression guard.

Update MCP tool docs in tools.py: (1) delete_memory docstring (line 264) — clarify that for Graphiti this deletes the edge/fact found by search, not the episode; mention delete_episode for episode removal. (2) search tool docstring — note that Graphiti results may have inferred category when category filtering is active. (3) FUSED_MEMORY_INSTRUCTIONS (line 47) — clarify that delete_memory deletes the item type returned by search (edges for Graphiti, memories for Mem0).

Write roundtrip test: test_search_then_delete_graphiti_roundtrip — mock a full search→delete workflow: configure graphiti.search to return edges with known UUIDs, call service.search(), take the first Graphiti result's id and source_store, pass them to service.delete_memory(), verify graphiti.remove_edge is called with the correct edge UUID. Validates the end-to-end contract is now internally consistent.
Run full test suite (pytest fused-memory/tests/) to verify all existing tests pass alongside new tests. Fix any regressions from the category filter change or delete_memory change.

design_decisions.rationale: Graphiti search returns EntityEdge UUIDs as the result ID. The MCP contract promises search result IDs work with delete_memory. Since search finds edges (facts), delete should delete edges. Episode deletion remains available via the dedicated delete_episode tool. This makes the search→delete contract internally consistent without adding new ID-type fields.

design_decisions.decision: Category filter uses GRAPHITI_PRIMARY set overlap to include Graphiti results
design_decisions.rationale: Graphiti edges don't carry category metadata — the category is only known at write time (for routing) and not stored on the edge itself. Rather than running expensive LLM classification at search time, we use the known routing table: if the user filters by any GRAPHITI_PRIMARY category (entities_and_relations, temporal_facts, decisions_and_rationale), Graphiti results with category=None pass through the filter. This prevents silent data loss (the current bug) while being truthful about what we know.

design_decisions.decision: Assign inferred category to Graphiti results only when unambiguous (single GRAPHITI_PRIMARY category in filter)
design_decisions.rationale: When a user filters by exactly one Graphiti-primary category, we can confidently assign it to the Graphiti results since they explicitly requested that specific category from a store that handles those types. When multiple GRAPHITI_PRIMARY categories are in the filter, we leave category as None to avoid false precision. This gives best-effort metadata without lying about what we know.

design_decisions.decision: No new fields added to MemoryResult model
design_decisions.rationale: The source_store field already disambiguates what the id represents (edge UUID for graphiti, memory UUID for mem0). Adding an id_type field would be redundant given the fix makes the existing contract work correctly. The simplest change that fixes the bug is preferred.

design_decisions.decision: backends module added to modules list beyond the original task spec
design_decisions.rationale: The GraphitiBackend (in backends/) needs a new remove_edge method to fix the delete contract. This is the minimal backend change needed. The alternative — putting edge deletion logic in MemoryService — would violate the existing layering where MemoryService delegates all Graphiti operations to GraphitiBackend.

reuse.what: GRAPHITI_PRIMARY / MEM0_PRIMARY category sets from enums.py
reuse.how: Already imported in memory_service.py (line 18). Use GRAPHITI_PRIMARY in the category filter logic to determine which Graphiti results to keep when category filtering is active.
Copy the exact pattern for the new remove_edge method, substituting EntityEdge for EpisodicNode. Same asyncio.wait_for timeout wrapper.

reuse.what: EntityEdge from graphiti_core with get_by_uuid and delete methods
reuse.how: Import EntityEdge and use get_by_uuid(driver, uuid) + edge.delete(driver) for edge removal.
Extend with uuid, source_node, target_node, episodes, valid_at, invalid_at fields to enable realistic Graphiti search result mocking in the new tests.
Add graphiti.remove_edge = AsyncMock() to the existing fixture. All new tests use this fixture for consistent mocking.
Follow the same structure (mock store returns, call search with categories, assert on results) but add Graphiti results to the mock setup to test the cross-store filtering behavior.
